recent_decisions.title: Approval of LPPI demerger and new partner funds
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the demerger of LPPI from LPPL and the admission of 7 new partner funds, with Lancashire County Council becoming a shareholder in a new holding company (TopCo). The decision includes a dividend in specie and delegation of authority to the Director of Finance and Commerce.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Dividend in specie and share subscriptions to be determined
key_data_points: 7 new partner funds to join LPPI pool
Lancashire County Council to become shareholder in TopCo
Dividend in specie to facilitate demerger
Delegation of authority to Director of Finance and Commerce

motions.text: That the Committee note the activity undertaken to date and the next stages in the process of demerging Local Pensions Partnership Investments Limited (LPPI) from Local Pensions Partnership Limited (LPPL) and admitting new Partner Funds into the LPPI pool.

That the Committee approve a dividend in specie as set out in the report to facilitate the demerger of LPPI from LPPL to a new holding company, (TopCo) subject to the agreement of Full Council on 12 March 2026 to amend the Articles of Association of LPPL to permit the payment of such a dividend.

That the Committee delegate authority to the Director of Finance and Commerce (S151 Officer), in consultation with the Monitoring Officer and the Head of the Pension Fund, to take all necessary steps to implement the dividend in specie referred to in b) above.

That the Committee recommend Full Council be asked to approve the demerger of Local Pensions Partnership Investments Limited (LPPI) from Local Pensions Partnership Limited (LPPL), with Lancashire County Council becoming a shareholder in a new holding company (TopCo), and the admission of 7 new Partner Funds into the LPPI pool as equal shareholders in TopCo.

recent_decisions.title: Exclusion of press and public
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee resolved to exclude the press and public from the meeting during consideration of the item on Local Pensions Partnership Investments Limited - Admission of New Partner Funds, citing exempt information under the Local Government Act 1972.
key_data_points: Exclusion based on exempt information under Local Government Act 1972
Public interest in maintaining exemption outweighed public interest in disclosure

motions.text: That the press and public be excluded from the meeting during consideration of the following item of business on the grounds that there would be a disclosure of exempt information as defined in paragraph 3 of Part 1 of Schedule 12A to the Local Government Act, 1972.

recent_decisions.committee: Environment, Economic Growth and Transport Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Recommendations on Connect to Work programme
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee asked the Cabinet Member to consider virementing funds between providers and requested a progress report on retention rates for different groups within 6-12 months.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Funding for the programme is dependent on government spending reviews.
key_data_points: 3500 participants expected by year 3
Current employment rate in Lancashire is 75.2%
43,000 people needed to reach national target of 80%

motions.text: The Cabinet Member for Economic Development and Growth be asked to consider the possibility of virementing money between procured providers of the Connect to Work programme to enable any underperforming areas to benefit.

A progress report outlining key performance indicators on retention rates for different group successes (veterans, disabled, long-term unemployed, youth unemployed) and the different areas of Lancashire be provided to the committee within 6 to 12 months' time.

recent_decisions.title: Granted planning permission for Woodnook Primary School outdoor classroom
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved planning permission for changing greenspace to an outdoor classroom at Woodnook Primary School, subject to conditions. 12 objections were received, and the decision was unanimous.
key_data_points: 12 objections received
Land owned by county council
Part of levelling up programme
Additional condition to remove permitted development rights

motions.text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in Appendix 'A' of the Committee report and the addition of condition 7.

recent_decisions.title: Deferred decision on Croston Road junction reconfiguration
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee deferred a decision on reconfiguring the Croston Road junction at Farington Moss due to outstanding issues. Five objections were received, and the decision was deferred for further consideration.
key_data_points: 5 objections received
Part of wider A582 dualing plans
Included in multiple public consultations
Involves signalised T-junctions and cycle tracks

motions.text: The committee deferred the decision on the application for further consideration.

recent_decisions.title: 2026/27 Budget Report Approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved the 2026/27 budget after rejecting all amendments. Progressive Lancashire (18-47), Conservative (18-50), Liberal Democrat (17-49), Labour (16-49), and Our West Lancashire (6-48) amendments all failed.
key_data_points: All amendments were rejected
Progressive Lancashire: 18 for, 47 against
Conservative: 18 for, 50 against
Liberal Democrat: 17 for, 49 against
Labour: 16 for, 49 against
Our West Lancashire: 6 for, 48 against

motions.text: Moved a recommendation on a Budget for 2026/27 as set out in the report.
Progressive Lancashire Group's Amendments to the Budget proposals.
Conservative Group's Amendment to the Budget proposals.
Liberal Democrat Group's Amendment to the Budget proposals.

Our West Lancashire Group's Amendment to the Budget proposals.

recent_decisions.title: Pension Fund Committee approves 110% funding buffer with 20-year recovery period
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee voted to retain a 110% funding buffer for the pension fund, reducing employer contributions from 17.1% to around 7.4%, with a 20-year recovery period to mitigate future risks. The decision was supported by a majority of members, balancing affordability and long-term stability.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Average employer contribution rate from April 2026
key_data_points: 110% funding buffer approved
20-year recovery period adopted
Employer contributions reduced from 17.1% to 7.4%
Current funding level is one-third over-funded

motions.text: That the Committee retain the current 110% buffer and the 16-year repayment period, consistent with proposals made at previous meetings.
That the reference to a 16-year recovery period be deleted and replaced with the 20-year recovery period as reflected in the modelling by the Actuary.

recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee resolved to exclude the press and public from the meeting during consideration of exempt information, citing public interest grounds.
key_data_points: Exclusion under Local Government Act 1972
Public interest in maintaining exemption outweighed disclosure
Exempt information as defined in paragraph 3 of Part 1 of Schedule 12A

motions.text: That the press and public be excluded from the meeting during consideration of the following item of business on the grounds that there would be a disclosure of exempt information as defined in paragraph 3 of Part 1 of Schedule 12A to the Local Government Act, 1972. It is considered that in all the circumstances the public interest in maintaining the exemption outweighs the public interest in disclosing the information.

recent_decisions.title: Approved allocation of £43.7m Adult Skills Fund and local flexibilities
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved the allocation of £43.7m for Adult Skills Fund and Free Courses for Jobs, including strategic flexibilities such as a £200 payment for learners starting Connect to Work or Skills Bootcamps. The vote was unanimous with no recorded vote counts.
